forecast_7day.short_forecast: Mostly Sunny then Slight Chance Showers And Thunderstorms
forecast_7day.detailed_forecast: A slight chance of showers and thunderstorms after 2pm. Mostly sunny, with a high near 80. Northeast wind around 2 mph. Chance of precipitation is 20%.

forecast_7day.short_forecast: Patchy Fog then Chance Showers And Thunderstorms
forecast_7day.detailed_forecast: Patchy fog before 10am, then a chance of showers and thunderstorms. Mostly sunny, with a high near 82. North northwest wind 1 to 5 mph. Chance of precipitation is 40%. New rainfall amounts between a tenth and quarter of an inch possible.

A chance of rain showers between 8am and 11am, then showers and thunderstorms likely between 11am and 4pm, then showers and thunderstorms. Partly sunny, with a high near 81. North wind around 2 mph. Chance of precipitation is 80%.

forecast_7day.short_forecast: Slight Chance Showers And Thunderstorms then Chance Showers And Thunderstorms
forecast_7day.detailed_forecast: A slight chance of showers and thunderstorms between 8am and 9am, then a chance of showers and thunderstorms between 9am and 2pm, then showers and thunderstorms likely between 2pm and 4pm, then showers and thunderstorms. Partly sunny, with a high near 79. Chance of precipitation is 80%.
A chance of showers and thunderstorms after 8am. Partly sunny, with a high near 80. Chance of precipitation is 40%.

forecast_7day.short_forecast: Mostly Sunny then Slight Chance Showers And Thunderstorms
forecast_7day.detailed_forecast: A slight chance of showers and thunderstorms after 2pm. Mostly sunny, with a high near 81.
A chance of showers and thunderstorms after 8am. Mostly sunny, with a high near 82. Chance of precipitation is 30%.

active_alerts.headline: Air Quality Alert issued July 15 at 2:44AM EDT by NWS Greenville-Spartanburg SC
active_alerts.description:
The North Carolina Department of Environmental Quality in Raleigh NC
has issued a Code Orange Air Quality Action Day for Ground Level
Ozone for Mecklenburg County, from 10 AM to 8 PM EDT Wednesday.

An Air Quality Action Day means that Ground Level Ozone
concentrations within the region may approach or exceed unhealthy
standards. For additional information, please visit the North
Carolina Division of Air Quality Web site at
https://airquality.climate.ncsu.edu/.
